description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a large, rectangular array of solar panels mounted on a raised platform. The panels are arranged in a grid and are tilted at an angle, likely to optimize sunlight capture. The structure includes a broad, flat base with distinct zones, possibly for maintenance access or electrical equipment, and is bordered by low walls on several sides. The surface beneath the panels is patterned, suggesting designated walkways or operational areas. The installation appears to be designed for urban integration, such as on a rooftop or within a dedicated solar park. The materials visible include metallic frames, glass or photovoltaic surfaces, and concrete or composite base elements.
description: A large, grid-arranged solar panel array on a raised, patterned platform with low perimeter walls and distinct operational zones.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the solar panel array and the patterned base, with the raised platform and low walls clearly visible.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the tilt of the solar panels and the depth of the installation, with the operational zones and wall structure more pronounced.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the length of the base, the solar panel array, and the arrangement of the patterned surface.
description_view_3: View from the rear, emphasizing the slope of the base, the grid of solar panels, and the labeled operational zones.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted on a raised platform with an aluminum frame and concrete base
attribute_array_configuration: single grid array of approximately 5x6 panels
